test_activity_planning_command_service.rationale: Command vocabularies and workflow grouping may become closed contract language; durable command services and session stores remain operations-owned.

test_backend_boundaries.rationale: DeploymentProgram stage and public boundary laws describe the operator-facing composition shape; core may retain descriptors and handoff contracts but not executable services.

test_contracts.rationale: The 23 mutation-holder laws are split-boundary laws: extracted core maps the closed EnvironmentContract / RuntimeContract / DerivedResource vocabulary, descriptor no-value/no-secret law, and operations-owned handoff in #791; operations / cpk-server must later prove executable holder mutation, publication, cleanup, rollback, retained-resource, and concurrency behavior.

test_current_graph_advancement.rationale: Lifecycle, admission, recovery, and advancement values may be closed contracts; durable enforcement, claims, locks, workers, and graph-store mutation remain operations-owned.

laws: Core may define pure operation contracts, descriptors, public command/read vocabulary, and service composition boundaries.
Core must not own FastAPI or MCP process startup, Docker execution, Postgres integration, worker loops, mutable stores, or coordinator effect dispatch.
API and MCP parity laws must share a single contract vocabulary rather than duplicate command models.
Transaction, store, schema, and mutation-holder laws remain operations or cpk-server handoff unless represented only as pure boundary descriptors.
Every live operations-contract family must have an explicit downstream mapping target before #740 closeout.
